description:
<title>Generate HTTP Status-Code Frequency Report Using sort & uniq</title>

    <description>
        You are an integration-platform developer double-checking that a new micro-service returns the expected mix of HTTP
        status codes.  
        
        1. A raw response capture already exists at  
           /home/user/api_test/logs/responses.log

        2. Your job is to produce a compact frequency report that counts how
           many times each status code occurs in that file.

        3. Create the report at  
           /home/user/api_test/reports/status_code_frequency.txt  
           (create the directory *reports* if it does not exist).

        4. The report **must be plain-text** and follow *exactly* this format so
           that automated grading can verify it:

           • Each line represents one distinct status code.  
           • Two space-separated columns per line:  
             1) the numeric status code,  
             2) the integer count of how many times that code appears in the
                original log.  
           • Lines **must** be ordered primarily by the count in
             descending numerical order (largest count first) and secondarily,
             when two counts are identical, by the status code itself in
             ascending numerical order.  
           • No leading or trailing spaces, no tabs, no header line, and end
             the file with a single UNIX newline.  

           A correct file will therefore look conceptually like:

           200 42  
           404 13  
           201 7  
           500 7  
           302 2

           (The actual numbers for this task will differ; the grader checks
           them.)

        5. Use classic command-line tools only (cut/awk, sort, uniq, etc.); **do
           not** open the file in an interactive editor.

        6. Keep the original
           /home/user/api_test/logs/responses.log
           unchanged.

        7. When you are finished, you can cat the resulting
           status_code_frequency.txt
           to the console so that a human reviewer sees the outcome, but the
           presence and exact contents of the file are what the automated tests
           will grade.

        Hint: the status code is the **third** whitespace-separated field on
        every line of responses.log.
    </description>

    <acceptance_criteria>
        The grader will check that:

        • /home/user/api_test/reports/status_code_frequency.txt exists.  
        • The file content follows *exactly* the two-column, space-separated,
          sorted format described above.  
        • The counts match what is present in /home/user/api_test/logs/responses.log.  
        • The original responses.log is not modified.
    </acceptance_criteria>

    <tools_restriction>
        You do not have sudo rights.  Standard POSIX utilities available in a
        minimal Linux environment are guaranteed to be present.
    </tools_restriction>
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing file the agent will read:
    -->
    <pre_existing_files>
        <file path="/home/user/api_test/logs/responses.log" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
2024-01-01T10:00:00Z GET /users 200
2024-01-01T10:00:01Z POST /users 201
2024-01-01T10:00:02Z GET /accounts 200
2024-01-01T10:00:03Z GET /users/123 404
2024-01-01T10:00:04Z GET /users 200
2024-01-01T10:00:05Z PUT /accounts/456 400
2024-01-01T10:00:06Z DELETE /users/789 200
2024-01-01T10:00:07Z GET /users 200
2024-01-01T10:00:08Z POST /login 400
2024-01-01T10:00:09Z GET /users 200
2024-01-01T10:00:10Z GET /accounts 500
2024-01-01T10:00:11Z GET /users 200
2024-01-01T10:00:12Z PATCH /accounts/456 500
2024-01-01T10:00:13Z POST /users 201
2024-01-01T10:00:14Z GET /transactions 400
]]></file>
    </pre_existing_files>

    <!--
        Target file the agent must create:
    -->
    <generated_files>
        <file path="/home/user/api_test/reports/status_code_frequency.txt" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
200 7
400 3
201 2
500 2
404 1
]]></file>
    </generated_files>

    <!--
        Directory that must exist by the end:
    -->
    <directories>
        <dir path="/home/user/api_test/reports" mode="0755"/>
    </directories>
